topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet of my evening. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake of air that seemed to seize in my chest. I clicked the link, and the words swam into focus: my piece, my carefully crafted analysis, published under a completely different name. A cold, creeping dread began to coil in my stomach, tightening with each line I scanned. My fingers trembled as I reread the byline, the unfamiliar name a grotesque stranger claiming my intellectual property. I stood up abruptly, knocking my chair backward with a clatter that echoed too loudly in the small apartment. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent city lights, my hands clenched so tightly my knuckles turned white.

[story 2]

It hit me like a physical blow when I saw the publication details. My stomach lurched, a sickening drop in my core. I reread the attribution multiple times, the words blurring into an incomprehensible mess. A wave of nausea washed over me, making my vision swim slightly. I dropped the tablet onto the worn rug, the sound muffled but final. I sank onto the floor, pulling my knees to my chest, wrapping my arms around myself as if to physically contain the sudden, overwhelming sense of violation. My mouth went dry; I couldn't form a coherent sound. Every muscle in my body felt rigid, vibrating with a frantic, trapped energy.

[story 3]

I stared at the screen, my mind refusing to process the reality unfolding before me. A slow, dreadful realization dawned, chilling the blood in my veins. It wasn't a mistake; it was a theft. A profound emptiness settled over me, vast and suffocating. I pressed my palms flat against my eyes, trying to physically block out the sight, but the image burned behind my lids. A low, ragged sound escaped my throat—a sound more animal than human. I backed away from the desk as if the screen itself were contaminated, stumbling until my back hit the cool plaster wall. I stood there, utterly still, shaking uncontrollably, the silence amplifying the frantic pounding of my own pulse.
